The intra-dataset results match the findings in Figure 2A: the CIForm framework consistently outperforms other computational methods across datasets with higher macro F1 scores.
The intra-dataset results match the findings in Figure 2B: the CIForm framework is able to annotate cell types with low appearance in the imbalanced dataset.
The intra-dataset results match the findings in Figure 2C: the CIForm framework outperforms current state-of-the-art methods on the Zheng68k dataset significantly.

The multiple reference results match the findings in Figure 4A: the accuracy of the CIForm framework outperforms other computational methods across Pancreas datasets with multiple reference sets and only four major cell types.
The multiple reference results match the findings in Figure 4B: the CIForm framework is more successful in categorising beta cells correctly.

The multiple reference results match the findings in Figure 5A and 5B: the accuracy and macro F1 score of the CIForm framework outperform other computational methods across Pancreas datasets with multiple reference sets and all cell types.
The multiple reference results match the findings in Figure 5C: the CIForm framework can successfully annotate both major and rare cell types.
